instruction: Read the text and choose the correct form of the verbs in the brackets to complete the gaps. Then, answer the question at the end.
body:
Last summer, Sarah (1. decide) ________ to travel across Europe. While she (2. explore) ________ the streets of Rome, she (3. meet) ________ an old friend from school. They (4. not see) ________ each other for ten years. Now, Sarah (5. live) ________ in Italy because she (6. work) ________ as a translator for a local company. She (7. usually / spend) ________ her weekends visiting museums. At the moment, she (8. learn) ________ how to cook authentic pasta. If she (9. have) ________ enough money next year, she will move to Florence. 

Question: Why is Sarah currently living in Italy? 
Answer: _________________________________________________

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple) and answer the questions.
body:
The Digital Nomad Life

Mark is a software developer who (1) ________ (work) from home these days. He (2) ________ (spend) most of his time in Bali, where he (3) ________ (enjoy) the tropical climate. However, his life wasn't always this flexible. Five years ago, he (4) ________ (have) a stressful office job in London. He remembers one specific Tuesday when, while he (5) ________ (prepare) a presentation for his boss, the power suddenly (6) ________ (go) out. That moment made him realize he needed a change.

Currently, Mark (7) ________ (develop) a new app for sustainable travel. He believes that in the future, more people (8) ________ (choose) this lifestyle. 

Questions:
9. What was Mark doing when the power cut happened in London?
10. Where does Mark live at the moment?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple) and answer the questions.
body:
The Unexpected Discovery

James is an archaeologist who (1) ________ (study) ancient civilizations. Usually, he (2) ________ (spend) his months in dusty libraries, but this year is different. Currently, he (3) ________ (conduct) an excavation in the jungles of Peru. 

Last month, while he (4) ________ (dig) near a hidden valley, he (5) ________ (find) a golden mask. He (6) ________ (not/expect) to discover anything so valuable. At that exact moment, his assistant (7) ________ (take) photographs of the site. James believes that this discovery (8) ________ (change) the history of the region forever.

Questions:
9. What is James doing in Peru this year?
10. What was the assistant doing when James found the mask?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. First, fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple). Then, answer the questions based on the text.
body:
The Life of a Marine Biologist

Dr. Elena Vance is a scientist who (1) ________ (study) ocean currents and coral reefs. She (2) ________ (believe) that protecting the deep sea is the most important challenge of our century. Currently, she (3) ________ (stay) on a research vessel near the Great Barrier Reef, where she (4) ________ (collect) water samples to analyze pollution levels.

Her passion for the ocean started years ago. When she was a teenager, she (5) ________ (spend) every summer at her grandfather's cottage by the coast. She remembers one particular afternoon ten years ago: while she (6) ________ (swim) in the shallow water, she (7) ________ (notice) a strange, injured sea turtle. She immediately called for help, and that experience changed her life. 

Next month, Dr. Vance (8) ________ (present) her latest findings at a global conference in Tokyo. She hopes that her data (9) ________ (convince) governments to create more protected marine areas.

Questions:
10. What was Dr. Vance doing when she noticed the injured sea turtle?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. First, fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple). Then, answer the questions based on the text.
body:
The Urban Gardener

Liam is a landscape architect who (1) ________ (design) green spaces for city dwellers. He (2) ________ (believe) that every balcony can become a small forest. Currently, he (3) ________ (work) on a massive project in New York, where he (4) ________ (transform) a grey rooftop into a community garden. 

His passion started years ago. When he was a student in Canada, he (5) ________ (volunteer) at a local farm. One afternoon, while he (6) ________ (plant) organic vegetables, he (7) ________ (realize) that urban areas desperately need more nature. He (8) ________ (not / have) much experience back then, but he was determined to learn.

Next year, Liam (9) ________ (publish) a book about vertical gardening. He hopes that his guide (10) ________ (inspire) thousands of people to plant trees in their apartments.

Questions:
1. What is Liam's profession and what is he doing in New York at the moment?
2. What happened while Liam was volunteering at the farm in Canada?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple) and answer the questions.
body:
The Mystery of the Old Clockmaker

Mr. Harrison is a retired watchmaker who (1) ________ (live) in a small village in the Cotswolds. He (2) ________ (own) a tiny shop where he repairs antique clocks. Most days, he (3) ________ (spend) his time cleaning gears and polishing gold frames. However, this week is unusual. Currently, he (4) ________ (prepare) for a prestigious exhibition in London. He (5) ________ (work) harder than usual because he wants everything to be perfect.

His passion for clocks started decades ago. In 1975, while he (6) ________ (study) engineering at university, he (7) ________ (discover) a broken 18th-century pocket watch in a flea market. He (8) ________ (spend) three months fixing it, and that experience changed his life. 

Mr. Harrison believes that traditional craftsmanship is disappearing. He hopes that his exhibition (9) ________ (inspire) young people to learn this art. 

Questions:
10. What was Mr. Harrison doing when he discovered the old pocket watch?
11. Why is he working harder than usual this week?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. First, fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple). Then, answer the questions based on the text.
body:
The Unexpected Discovery

Oliver is a dedicated historian who (1) ________ (specialize) in medieval architecture. He (2) ________ (believe) that old buildings hold secrets about the people who lived centuries ago. Currently, he (3) ________ (analyze) some ancient blueprints of a castle in Scotland to prepare for his new book.

Last winter, while Oliver (4) ________ (explore) a ruined chapel in the Highlands, he (5) ________ (notice) a loose stone in the wall. He (6) ________ (pull) it out and found a hidden diary from the 14th century. At that moment, it (7) ________ (rain) heavily, so he quickly hid the book under his coat.

Now, Oliver (8) ________ (translate) the diary into English. He is sure that this document (9) ________ (reveal) a forgotten political scandal of the Middle Ages. 

Questions:
10. What was Oliver doing when he found the hidden diary?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks. First, fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, or Future Simple). Then, answer the question at the end.
body:
The Mystery of the Northern Lights

Clara is a professional astronomer who (1) ________ (study) solar activity and its effect on Earth. She (2) ________ (believe) that the universe still holds many secrets. Currently, she (3) ________ (stay) in a remote observatory in Norway, where she (4) ________ (monitor) the aurora borealis for a new research project. 

Her passion for space started years ago. In 2012, while she (5) ________ (attend) a science camp in Canada, she (6) ________ (see) a rare celestial event that changed her perspective on science. At that moment, most of her classmates (7) ________ (sleep), but Clara stayed awake and watched the sky for hours. 

Now, she is working on a theory about magnetic storms. She is sure that her next discovery (8) ________ (help) scientists predict solar flares more accurately. 

Question: What was Clara doing in 2012 when she witnessed the celestial event?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks below. Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Past Simple, Present Continuous, Past Continuous, or Present Perfect) and answer the questions.
body:
The Unexpected Journey of a Chef

Julian is a professional chef who (1) ________ (specialize) in molecular gastronomy. He (2) ________ (believe) that cooking is a form of art. Currently, he (3) ________ (stay) in a small village in Provence, where he (4) ________ (experiment) with local herbs for his new menu. 

However, his path to success was not easy. Ten years ago, while he (5) ________ (study) at a culinary school in Paris, he (6) ________ (experience) a total failure during his final exam. While he (7) ________ (prepare) a complex soufflé, the oven suddenly (8) ________ (stop) working. He (9) ________ (not / manage) to finish the dish on time, but that failure taught him resilience. 

Since then, Julian (10) ________ (travel) to over twenty countries to learn different techniques. He is convinced that his passion will inspire others.

Questions:
1. What is Julian doing in Provence at the moment?
2. What happened while Julian was preparing his soufflé in Paris?

instruction: Read the text and complete the tasks. First, fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets (Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, or Past Continuous). Then, answer the questions based on the text.
body:
The Mystery of the Silent Forest

Arthur is a wildlife photographer who (1) ________ (specialize) in capturing rare birds. He (2) ________ (believe) that patience is the most important tool for any photographer. Currently, he (3) ________ (stay) in a remote cabin in the mountains of Montana, where he (4) ________ (document) the migration patterns of golden eagles.

However, his passion started much earlier. Twenty years ago, while he (5) ________ (walk) through a dense forest in Alaska, he (6) ________ (spot) a bird that scientists thought was extinct. At that moment, the wind (7) ________ (blow) strongly, and he almost dropped his camera. That experience changed his life and led him to professional photography.

Now, Arthur (8) ________ (work) on a new photo album. He hopes that his work (9) ________ (encourage) people to protect wild habitats. He is convinced that nature (10) ________ (need) our immediate help.

Questions:
1. What is Arthur doing in Montana at the moment?
2. What happened while Arthur was walking in Alaska twenty years ago?
